questions.explanation: class is sugar over constructor functions + prototypes.
Classes must be called with new — otherwise TypeError.
super.hi() calls the parent's method; then we append 'B'.

questions.prompt: In a subclass constructor, `super(...)` must be called BEFORE:
options: Only if the parent has a constructor
questions.explanation: Accessing this before super() throws ReferenceError.
Class fields initialize on instance creation. Two inc calls → 2.
#items is a private field — accessed only inside the class.
Static methods are on the CLASS itself (like a factory).

options: Methods declared with method() { } are own instance properties
Methods are on Class.prototype and shared by instances
questions.explanation: Class methods live on the prototype — one copy shared by all instances.
